$comment: ExpertMachina scope seed, corrected against the REAL repo layout (validated 2026-07-09 via the first profile-mode scoping pass, WS-finance-cost-leakage-workbench). The Steward copies this to handoffs/current-scope.json, fills every <PLACEHOLDER>, and may tighten — never weaken — the pre-filled policy fields. All globs are relative to targetRepo. Standing forbiddenGlobs and the constitutional proofs below mirror CLAUDE.profile.md.
$commentIsolation: targetCanonical is the user's ORIGINAL checkout — READ-ONLY to agents (fetch/verify/clone-source only). All Builder/Sweeper edits happen in targetWorktree (the 'ExpertMachina copy for EM' isolated copy) on a feature branch; allowed/forbidden globs are relative to that copy. Constitution: Target isolation.
validatedAgainst: <origin/main SHA the Steward inspected AFTER a fetch — never the local working tree alone. The Builder re-fetches and re-verifies this SHA at start; if origin/main has moved, Builder stops and returns to the Steward for re-validation (lesson of WS-finance-cost-leakage: the target ships through its own pipeline concurrently)>
objective: <one-paragraph objective. For a new workbench: name the domain_key, the diagnostic value, and confirm it follows the Workbench Catalog v1 bundle pattern>
humanApproval: PENDING — Builder must not start until a human replaces this value with who/where approval was given
preconditions: Builder fetches origin and verifies origin/main still equals validatedAgainst; if moved, STOP and return to Steward for re-validation
Builder starts from a fresh branch off up-to-date origin/main — never on top of a test/feature branch
For workbench work: all skill contracts the workbench declares must be ratified ACTIVE before the runner will accept them (Catalog v1 convention)
nonGoals: No changes to the governed backend (backend/app/**) — no new endpoints, projection types, connectors, policy, identity, or ledger logic
No changes to the MCP gateway or backend/mcp_server.py
No new fact approval states, evidence classes, or clearance levels
No vault writes and no changes to the untouchable floor (vault 00_system, 07_agent_workspaces, 08_proposals)
No real customer data in any corpus — synthetic/example documents only
$commentForbidden: backend/app/** is the governed core (ledger, policy, identity, proposals, projections engine). Shipped workbenches are each other's forbidden territory — a new workbench never edits a shipped one; when a NEW workbench ships, add its path to this standing list (refreshed 2026-07-09 against origin/main 7c2203b: six shipped). workbench/common.py is the ruling-6 shared plumbing: REUSE BY IMPORT is the sanctioned pattern (its stability is tracked release over release); editing it is forbidden from workbench workstreams. Until a shipped-workbench-extension policy is minted by its own Steward design pass, workstreams target GREENFIELD catalog slots only.
projectionOnlyRule: The workbench consumes ONLY the existing doors: .empkg packages via the package channel and MCP path/neighbor/subgraph queries at a real AGENT token's clearance. It never reads backend internals, the ledger, or raw stores directly. Data with no existing projection/package door = BLOCKED finding for the Steward, never a reason to reach deeper.
canonicalMutationPolicy: Zero direct canonical writes — constitutional, not configurable (D29 One-Way Valve). Findings re-enter exclusively through the proposal lane (vault 08_proposals -> PROPOSAL-lane connector -> CANDIDATE -> human gate -> DERIVED fact). Proposal-lane candidates are never auto-approved. The fifth permanent guard must still prove from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts.

proofRequirements.mustShow: corpus documents load, skill contracts parse and are ACTIVE, workbench.yaml is catalog-valid
each declared skill produces evidence-cited findings on the seeded corpus; refusal-first on unsupported questions (profile rule 1)
explicit refusal/no-evidence output when the corpus lacks support — never fabricated findings; at least one such case per skill contract (profile rule 1)
the existing fifth permanent guard still proves from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts (profile rules 3-5, D29)
clearance filtering intact plus the workbench's denied-access case: explicit refusal under insufficient clearance (profile rule 7)
no regressions vs a baseline run recorded BEFORE the first Builder edit (baseline output pasted in Builder handoff)

verificationGate.$comment: See profiles/expertmachina/workflows/evidence-gate.md. A provenance or permission fail is never waivable by vote count — it escalates to a human.

rollbackAnchor: git tag ws-<domain_key>-baseline on the Builder's branch, created before the first edit
stopConditions: Builder verifies preconditions first and stops if unmet
Builder stops when all proofs have run with literal evidence and the handoff (incl. profile addendum A1-A5) is written; does not merge
Builder stops immediately if any needed data lacks an existing projection/package door (BLOCKED finding to Steward)
Sweeper stops if the baseline suite is red
Any agent stops immediately if work requires editing a forbiddenGlob
Any agent stops immediately upon finding a write path from workbench code to canonical state (critical defect, straight to human)

downstreamPlan.part: workbench bundle: workbench.yaml, ratified skill contracts, synthetic corpus, acceptance runner (customer_operations as reference)
corpus + acceptance tests incl. refusal and denied-access cases
